# Issue #723 EC section verbatim — Production deployment docs consolidation

## Exit criteria / Validation

- [ ] **EC-1**: `docs/deployment/production.md` exists, is <500 lines, and covers all 8 gap topics from the audit (macOS version, Apple Silicon, Docker Desktop floor, launchd install, PROD_HOST clarification, data init/backup, firewall/network, TLS/reverse-proxy) — *Verified by*: a checklist in the PR description ticking off each gap topic with a doc-section anchor + `wc -l docs/deployment/production.md` < 500.
- [ ] **EC-2**: `prod/README.md` is <15 lines and contains no operational content that lives in `docs/deployment/production.md` — *Verified by*: `wc -l prod/README.md` + a manual diff vs the new docs.
- [ ] **EC-3**: From `AGENTS.md` and `README.md`, every relevant prod doc is reachable in ≤2 clicks — *Verified by*: manual walk-through documented in the PR description; doc-graph test (from #718 Phase 2, if merged) green.
- [ ] **EC-4**: `bash deploy/install-prod.sh --check-only` on the existing prod Mac exits 0 with a clean "ready" summary; on a deliberately-broken environment (older Docker, wrong arch) it exits non-zero with a clear actionable error — *Human-only*: requires a real macOS machine; cannot be run in CI (Linux runners) — *Evidence*: terminal transcripts attached to the Phase 2 PR.
- [ ] **EC-5**: A fresh-Mac dry run: the owner follows `docs/deployment/production.md` only — no greps, no Slack questions — and either succeeds end-to-end or reports the exact step that was unclear — *Human-only*: requires a clean macOS environment and real credentials — *Evidence*: a comment on this issue with the result before closing.
